description.en: A subclass of Action for handling boolean flags with positive and negative options. Adding a single argument such as --foo automatically creates both --foo and --no-foo options, storing True and False respectively: >>> import argparse >>> parser = argparse.ArgumentParser() >>> parser.add_argument('--foo', action=argparse.BooleanOptionalAction) >>> parser.parse_args(['--no-foo']) Namespace(foo=False) Added in version 3.9.

excerpt.es: A subclass of Action for handling boolean flags with positive and negative options. Adding a single argument such as --foo automatically creates both --foo and --no-foo options, storing True and False...
excerpt.en: A subclass of Action for handling boolean flags with positive and negative options. Adding a single argument such as --foo automatically creates both --foo and --no-foo options, storing True and False...
